name: United States v. W. M. Webb, Inc.
question: Are boat captains and crewmen in independent boats engaged in contractual agreements with commercial fishing companies “employees” of those companies under the Federal Insurance Contributions Act and the Federal Unemployment Tax Act?
description: A case in which the Court held that boat captains and crewmen in independent boats engaged in contractual agreements with commercial fishing companies are “employees” of those companies under the Federal Insurance Contributions Act and the Federal Unemployment Tax Act
